text.title: OpenAI API Endpoints and Model Selection
text.summary: Key endpoints, model selection matrix, Responses API vs Chat Completions, and rate limit tiers.

list.item: Chat Completions (/v1/chat/completions): text generation, conversation, vision, function calling
Responses API (/v1/responses): stateful API (2025+); manages conversation history server-side via previous_response_id
Embeddings (/v1/embeddings): text-embedding-3-large for RAG, semantic search
Batch (/v1/batches): 50% cost discount, 24h completion window, JSONL input
Audio (/v1/audio/transcriptions, /v1/audio/speech): Whisper for STT, TTS models

rule.statement: Default model: gpt-4o for quality-sensitive tasks; gpt-4o-mini for classification, routing, and formatting (90% cheaper). Use o3-mini for complex reasoning. Always pin exact version: `gpt-4o-2024-08-06` not `gpt-4o`.
rule.rationale: Non-pinned model aliases change behavior on OpenAI's update schedule without warning. A silent model upgrade can break prompt-sensitive production systems.

rule.statement: For new projects, use the Responses API over Chat Completions. Use `previous_response_id` for multi-turn conversations — it eliminates manual history management and reduces payload size.
rule.rationale: Chat Completions requires the developer to send the full conversation history on every call. The Responses API manages history server-side, reducing payload size and developer surface area. OpenAI is actively developing the Responses API; Chat Completions is in maintenance mode.

rule.statement: Use `client.beta.chat.completions.parse(response_format=MyModel)` instead of JSON mode. Structured Outputs guarantee schema compliance; JSON mode only guarantees valid JSON syntax.
rule.rationale: JSON mode failures are silent: valid JSON with missing or wrong-typed fields passes JSON parsing but fails downstream. Native Structured Outputs enforce the schema at the model level.

example.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel from openai import OpenAI
class Response(BaseModel): answer: str confidence: float
client = OpenAI() result = client.beta.chat.completions.parse( model="gpt-4o-2024-08-06", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "..."}], response_format=Response, ) typed_result: Response = result.choices[0].message.parsed

text.reference: Exponential backoff for rate limit errors
